title: Computer system and equipment installers and servicers
description: Research, design, develop, or test computer or computer-related equipment for commercial, industrial, military, or scientific use. May supervise the manufacturing and installation of computer or computer-related equipment and components. Research, design, develop, or test electronic components and systems for commercial, industrial, military, or scientific use employing knowledge of electronic theory and materials properties. Design electronic circuits and components for use in fields such as telecommunications, aerospace guidance and propulsion control, acoustics, or instruments and controls. Design and implement radio frequency identification device (RFID) systems used to track shipments or goods. (Based on 10 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Provide input into the design and manufacturing of new equipment, and prepare engineering sketches or specifications for construction, relocation, or installation of equipment, facilities, products, or systems.

Access specific areas to install terminal boxes, auxiliary equipment, or appliances, using bucket trucks, climbing poles or ladders, or entering tunnels, trenches, or crawl spaces. Dig holes or trenches as necessary for equipment installation and access, and lay underground cable directly in trenches or string it through conduits running through trenches.

Install, maintain, or repair audio and visual electronic reception equipment or accessories at customers' premises, and install telephone station equipment and related apparatus such as intercommunication systems and telephone booths.

Inspect or test lines or cables, recording and analyzing test results to assess transmission characteristics and locate faults or malfunctions. Test connections to ensure that power supplies are adequate and that communications links function, and test circuits and components of telecommunications equipment to isolate sources of malfunctions, using test meters and other tools.

Splice cables and measure signal strength at utility poles using electronic test equipment, and clean or maintain tools or test equipment. Also, clean switches and replace contact points, and perform routine maintenance on equipment, including adjusting and lubricating components.

String cables, place insulation over conductors, or seal splices with moisture-proof covering. Transport equipment to work sites and pull up cable by hand from large reels mounted on trucks. Pull cable through ducts by hand or winches, and determine site locations and construction requirements through observation and communication with customers.

Perform database verifications using computers, and maintain computer and manual records pertaining to facilities and equipment. Keep records of work orders and test and maintenance reports, and complete reports related to project status or other work details, using computer software.

Assess the operation of systems and devices related to radio frequency identification (RFID) technology: install, test, maintain RFID systems and document equipment or process details associated with RFID technology. Train users in system operation and provide technical support for RFID technology.

Evaluate factors such as reporting formats required and need for security restrictions to determine hardware configuration. Specify power supply requirements and configuration, and select appropriate hardware and materials ensuring compliance with specifications and product requirements.

Assemble and install communication equipment, including data and telephone lines, wiring, switching equipment, and computer systems. Repair or replace faulty equipment and disassemble entertainment equipment to repair or replace components, wiring, and ensure proper functioning.

Collaborate with other workers to locate and correct malfunctions. Communicate with customers to determine the nature of problems or explain repairs, and instruct customers on the safe and proper use of equipment. Demonstrate equipment to customers and explain its use while responding to inquiries or complaints.

Install and repair electronic equipment or instruments such as televisions, radios, and audio equipment. Test repaired or newly installed equipment to ensure it meets specifications and functions properly.
Run appropriate power, ground, or coaxial cables, and replace existing antennas with new ones as directed. Adjust or modify equipment based on performance needs and integrative requirements.
Stay updated with rapid advancements in computer technology; read literature, attend meetings or conferences to remain abreast of industry research and developments.

Perform site analysis to determine system configurations and processes impacted by technological implementation. Fill machines and test various equipment functions, including evaluating equipment performance records to assess functioning.

Install updated software and programs that maintain existing software while providing new features. Diagnose problems from remote locations, and refer to manufacturers' manuals to obtain maintenance instructions for specific malfunctions.
Maintain communication regarding procedures and specific malfunctions, while also requesting support from technical service centers when on-site procedures do not resolve issues.
Prepare, review, or maintain maintenance schedules, design documentation, or operational reports related to systems and equipment.

Perform maintenance or repair work on existing equipment using hand or power tools, and determine viability of sites through observation and discussions with customers regarding construction requirements.

Clean, oil, or adjust mechanical parts to maintain machines' operating efficiency and to prevent breakdowns; disassemble machines to examine parts for wear or defects; operate machines to test functioning of parts or mechanisms; assemble and reassemble machines according to specifications.

Complete repair bills, shop records, time cards, or expense reports; prepare budgets or cost estimates for equipment, construction, or installation projects; compute estimates and actual costs of factors such as materials, labor, or outside contractors.

Provide technical support or instruction to staff or customers regarding electronics equipment standards; advise customers concerning equipment operation, maintenance, or programming; confer with customers to determine details of equipment problems.

Recommend repair or design modifications of electronics components or systems, based on factors such as environment, service, cost, or system capabilities; analyze electronics system requirements, capacity, cost, or customer needs to determine project feasibility; evaluate project work to ensure effectiveness, technical adequacy, or compatibility in the resolution of complex electronics engineering problems.

Operate computer-assisted engineering or design software or equipment to perform electronics engineering tasks; research or develop new green electronics technologies; develop or implement electronic maintenance programs or computer information management systems.

Confer with engineers, customers, vendors, or others to discuss existing or potential electronics engineering projects or products; inspect electronic equipment, instruments, products, or systems to ensure conformance to specifications, safety standards, or applicable codes; prepare documentation containing information such as confidential descriptions or specifications of proprietary hardware or software.

Test components or circuits of faulty equipment to locate defects; align, adjust, or calibrate equipment according to specifications; update existing equipment, performing tasks such as installing updated circuit boards or additional memory; install and configure new equipment, including software or peripherals; test new systems to ensure they are in working order; reinstall software programs or adjust settings on existing software to fix machine malfunctions; reassemble and test equipment after repairs; locate and remove or repair circuit defects; inspect and test electrical systems and equipment to find malfunctions.

Maintain parts inventories and order additional parts needed for repairs; maintain records of equipment maintenance work or repairs; maintain equipment service records; monitor tool and part inventories and the condition and maintenance of shops; determine project material or equipment needs; requisition materials and supplies, such as tools, equipment, or replacement parts; determine schedules, sequences, and assignments for work activities, based on work priority, quantity of equipment, and skill of personnel.

Analyze information to determine needed installations, services, or repairs; investigate accidents or injuries and prepare reports; conduct or arrange for worker training in safety, repair, or maintenance techniques; monitor employees' work levels and review work performance; recommend or initiate personnel actions, such as hires, promotions, transfers, discharges, or disciplinary measures.

Inspect and monitor work areas, examine tools and equipment, and provide employee safety training to prevent, detect, and correct unsafe conditions or safety rule violations; represent employer at conferences or meetings.

Investigate green consumer electronics applications for consumer electronic devices; measure, cut, and install frameworks and conduit to support and connect wiring; install electrical equipment such as air-conditioning or ignition systems; repair or rebuild equipment such as starters, generators, or door controls; adjust, repair, or replace defective wiring and relays in systems; install fixtures, outlets, terminal boards, switches, and wall boxes; splice wires and solder connections; develop, implement, or evaluate maintenance policies and procedures.

Travel to customers' stores or offices to service machines or provide emergency repair service; meet with vendors or suppliers to discuss products used in repair work; review, evaluate, accept, and coordinate completion of work bids from contractors; compile operational or personnel records.
